nodes.name: candidate-playbook.md
nodes.summary: Promoted CI failure diagnosis playbook (candidate id: log-backed-minimal-fix) defining the accepted and rejected paths for diagnosing GitHub Actions failures, requiring captured log evidence, scope comparison, and a named local regression command before any fix can be merged.

nodes.name: report.json
nodes.summary: Evaluator-RAG prototype run report for the ci-failure-diagnosis scenario, recording a prototype_passed result with per-dimension scores and findings that warn against rerun-only approaches and require log-backed regression gates.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a versioned schema (ecc.evaluator-rag.report.v1) and read_only flag to indicate these are immutable prototype artifacts.

nodes.name: scenario.json
nodes.summary: Evaluator-RAG scenario definition for CI failure diagnosis in read-only prototype mode, specifying retrieval sources (gh run/pr commands, repo docs, tests), five retrieval questions, forbidden actions, and acceptance gates that must be satisfied before a playbook is promoted.
nodes.languageNotes: Acceptance gates and forbidden_actions form a contract that the verifier checks against candidate playbooks.

nodes.name: trace.json
nodes.summary: Evaluator-RAG execution trace for the ci-failure-diagnosis prototype run, capturing the five sequential phases (observation, retrieval, proposal, verification, promotion) with evidence pointers and the final promoted candidate id.
nodes.languageNotes: The read_only flag signals this trace must not be modified; it is an immutable record of a past prototype run.

nodes.name: verifier-result.json
nodes.summary: Verifier output for the ci-failure-diagnosis scenario comparing two candidates: accepts log-backed-minimal-fix (score 0.93) for requiring concrete evidence and rejecting rerun-only-green-wait (score 0.17) for failing to identify the failure cause or add a regression guard.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides scored, reasoned decisions per candidate with rollback instructions, forming the authoritative promotion record.
